business_angle: AC Painters & Decorators are the Bootle and Crosby team who paint with colour confidence - the bold feature wall, the boutique-hotel finish, the wallpaper and the furniture and shelving brought back to life - inside and out, across north Merseyside.
why_this_trader_wins: The reviews keep landing on the same thing: this is a painter people trust with strong, deliberate colour, not just magnolia. Sylvia's tired shelving unit became a 'luxurious boutique hotel look' in olive green; another customer had bedroom paint and wallpaper hung; Helen had the exterior of a terrace house in Crosby painted. Combined with a clean, tidy, on-time way of working that runs through every review, the angle is bold-colour interiors and feature finishes done to a high standard, not generic decorating.
target_customer: Bootle, Crosby and north-Merseyside homeowners who want a room, a feature wall, a piece of furniture or the front of a period or terrace house painted with real colour and a proper finish - and who want tidy, on-time tradespeople they would have back.
page_single_job: Get that homeowner to request a quote (or call) for an interior, feature-colour, wallpaper, furniture-refinishing or exterior painting job.

customer_praise_themes.theme: bold, deliberate colour and a boutique-hotel finish
customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[2] (Sylvia): 'from a bit dated looking shelves unit into a luxurious boutique hotel look! ... The olive green paint and finish looks stunning'

customer_praise_themes.theme: wallpaper hanging and full room decorating
customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[1] (N): 'bedroom paint / wallpaper, communication was really good, excellent finish'

customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[2] (Sylvia): 'a bit dated looking shelves unit into a luxurious boutique hotel look'

customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[0] (nic): 'Turned up on time each day, kept everything tidy ... Managed to fit me in at short notice'; reviews[1] (N): 'cleaned all up after'; reviews[3] (Gemma): 'tidied up after themselves'

customer_praise_themes.theme: Chris and the team, easy and straightforward to deal with
customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[1] (N): 'Chris and the lads were great'; reviews[3] (Gemma): 'Chris and the lads'; reviews[4] (Helen): 'Chris and his team at AC Painters'; reviews[0] (nic): 'genuinely nice, straightforward guys'

named_people.name: Chris
named_people.role: lead decorator / proprietor named in reviews
named_people.basis: brief.json contact_name_usage_allowed=true; 'Chris' is named in 3 separate Google reviews (reviews[1] N 'Chris and the lads', reviews[3] Gemma 'Chris and the lads', reviews[4] Helen 'Chris and his team at AC Painters') - high confidence, evidence_count=3. This clears the skill's bar (named in >=2 review bodies). The business name is 'AC Painters & Decorators' (not 'Chris's...'), so use Chris in a light third person tied to the named team ('Chris and the team'), never invent a surname or an owner backstory.

strongest_review_quote.text: Wow from a bit dated looking shelves unit into a luxurious boutique hotel look! I love it! The olive green paint and finish looks stunning! Super professional, excellent price. Definitely recommend.
strongest_review_quote.author: Sylvia

claims_to_avoid: any certification or membership (Dulux Select, Painting & Decorating Association, Checkatrade Vetted, TrustATrader, Which? Trusted Trader) - none verified; directory_probes show 0 verified profiles (Yell/MyBuilder/Trustpilot are snippet-only exact-name-single-signal; TrustATrader was an identity homonym and rejected; checkatrade/rated_people/bark/houzz/myjobquote NOT_FOUND)
Google Places junk: brief.json services[] are all the same niche label ('Painting & decorating' x3 from google/name/niche) - use the real evidenced service set instead of repeating the generic label
the street 'Springs Cl' / full address 'Springs Cl, Liverpool, Bootle L20 3UA' anywhere public - use Bootle / L20 and the north-Merseyside framing only

service areas beyond Bootle, Crosby, Liverpool and north Merseyside - Crosby is evidenced (reviews[4] Helen); the rest are UK-geographic derivations from the L20 (Bootle) base and must stay plausible for a small team (no national footprint)
owner surname or backstory - only the first name 'Chris' and 'the lads / the team' are evidenced
an owner photo, logo or brand colours - none found (brief.json brand.colours empty, logo null)
a photo count as a stat, or any review/photo-count fabrication
personality: warm, confident with colour, tidy and straightforward; a small team you would happily have back in the house
evidence_strength: moderate-content / single-source (5 specific Google reviews carrying a clear bold-colour angle + 4.9/58 Google; only 3 usable photos, of which one is a finished interior and two are mid-renovation; no verified third-party profile, no Facebook/Instagram)
pitch_hook_summary: Lead on colour confidence and the boutique-feature finish (olive-green feature, boutique-hotel shelving, wallpaper, plus exterior terrace/period houses around Crosby and Bootle), backed by 4.9/58 Google.

metadata.location_note: enrich raised LOCATION_MISMATCH_NEEDS_REVIEW because the Google address city (Bootle) differs from the prospect region label (Liverpool). These are the same metro: Bootle (L20) is a town in the Liverpool/Merseyside urban area, and Crosby (evidenced in reviews[4]) sits just north of it. No real mismatch; coverage is framed as Bootle, Crosby, Liverpool and north Merseyside. This is a BUILD run; pitch_gate enforces at outreach time, not build time.
metadata.website_note: Existing site is BROKEN_OR_BAD_SITE (sitelift.site/acpaintersdecorators - a thin auto-generated sitelift page; enrich has_real_website=false, truly_no_website=true). Genuine rebuild target, NOT HAS_REAL_SITE.
metadata.manual_review_note: Single-source (Google-only): no verified third-party profile and contactability=NEEDS_MANUAL_REVIEW (no email, UK mobile only, WhatsApp not auto-checked per the OpenWA teardown - gather printed 'WhatsApp checked: no'). Same posture as the Damo and Kyle Knowles reference builds; the evidence is specific and usable.
metadata.authored_blind: Path B authored from frozen evidence + CLAUDE.md + the webfortrades-site-design skill only. Distinct from Kyle (wet-slate + viridian teal; Bricolage + Instrument), Damo (blackened iron + cedar amber; Oswald + Hanken), library pairings, and the two parallel painter builds (sage+pewter, white+eucalyptus+oak). This build uses a deep aubergine/damson + warm brass + bone-white boutique palette with Bodoni Moda + Mulish, grounded in the trader's own bold-colour, boutique-finish world.
